title: Rootless Podman requires /etc/subuid to be sorted with multiple --uidmap
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)** /kind bug **Description** I'm trying to run a rootless container with some rather complex UID/GID maps. I want to map … * host uid 100000-165535 to container uid 0-65535, * the executing user (user `test`, uid 1000) to container uid 65536, and * host uid 975 to container uid 65537. I looks like rootless Podman (or the kernel?) requires `/etc/subuid` and `/etc/subgid` resp. `/proc/self/uid_map` and `/proc/self/gid_map` to be sorted for complex UID/GID maps like the above. See "Additional information" below: By either running as root, or by simply changing order, `podman run` succeeds. **Steps to reproduce the issue:** 1. Update `/etc/subuid` and `/etc/subgid` to include the necessary UID ranges: ``` test:100000:65536 test:975:1 ``` 2. Execute `podman run` with appropriate `--uidmap` and `--gidmap` options: ``` $ podman run -dt --name "test" \ --uidmap 0:1:65536 --uidmap 65536:0:1 --uidmap 65537:65537:1 \ --gidmap 0:1:65536 --gidmap 65536:0:1 --gidmap 65537:65537:1 \ docker.io/busybox ``` **Describe the results you received:** `podman run` fails with the following error: ``` Error: writing file `/proc/3810/gid_map`: Operation not permitted: OCI permission denied ``` **Describe the results you expected:** `podman run` succeeds. **Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):** There are two things to note: 1. If we run `podman run` as root (i.e. a rootful container), `podman run` succeeds. The UID/GID maps are applied just fine. 2. If we swap lines in `/etc/subuid` and `/etc/subgid`, and update the `--uidmap` and `--gidmap` options accordingly, `podman run` (rootless) suceeds: 1. Update `/etc/subuid` and `/etc/subgid` to include the necessary UID ranges, just the other way round: ``` test:975:1 test:100000:65536 ``` 2. Execute `podman run` with updated `--uidmap` and `--gidmap` options: ``` $ podman run -dt --name "test" \ --uidmap 0:2:65536 --uidmap 65536:0:1 --uidmap 65537:1:1 \ --gidmap 0:2:65536 --gidmap 65536:0:1 --gidmap 65537:1:1 \ docker.io/busybox ```
Output of podman version: ``` Version: 3.1.2 API Version: 3.1.2 Go Version: go1.16.3 Git Commit: 51b8ddbc22cf5b10dd76dd9243924aa66ad7db39 Built: Wed Apr 21 23:34:03 2021 OS/Arch: linux/amd64 ```
Output of podman info --debug: ``` host: arch: amd64 buildahVersion: 1.20.1 cgroupManager: systemd cgroupVersion: v2 conmon: package: /usr/bin/conmon is owned by conmon 1:2.0.28-1 path: /usr/bin/conmon version: 'conmon version 2.0.28, commit: 6b18f7e0f2e4cd7f7b016b88141e82210d370008' cpus: 3 distribution: distribution: arch version: unknown eventLogger: journald hostname: SGS-CPX21-Nuernberg-1 idMappings: gidmap: null uidmap: null kernel: 5.10.37-1-lts linkmode: dynamic memFree: 3686375424 memTotal: 4019585024 ociRuntime: name: crun package: /usr/bin/crun is owned by crun 0.19.1-1 path: /usr/bin/crun version: |- crun version 0.19.1 commit: 1535fedf0b83fb898d449f9680000f729ba719f5 spec: 1.0.0 +SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +YAJL os: linux remoteSocket: path: /run/podman/podman.sock security: apparmorEnabled: false capabilities: CAP_CHOWN,CAP_DAC_OVERRIDE,CAP_FOWNER,CAP_FSETID,CAP_KILL,CAP_NET_BIND_SERVICE,CAP_SETFCAP,CAP_SETGID,CAP_SETPCAP,CAP_SETUID,CAP_SYS_CHROOT rootless: false seccompEnabled: true selinuxEnabled: false slirp4netns: executable: "" package: "" version: "" swapFree: 8589930496 swapTotal: 8589930496 uptime: 14h 46m 3.63s (Approximately 0.58 days) registries: {} store: configFile: /etc/containers/storage.conf containerStore: number: 1 paused: 0 running: 0 stopped: 1 graphDriverName: overlay graphOptions: overlay.mountopt: nodev graphRoot: /var/lib/containers/storage graphStatus: Backing Filesystem: extfs Native Overlay Diff: "false" Supports d_type: "true" Using metacopy: "true" imageStore: number: 13 runRoot: /run/containers/storage volumePath: /var/lib/containers/storage/volumes version: APIVersion: 3.1.2 Built: 1619040843 BuiltTime: Wed Apr 21 23:34:03 2021 GitCommit: 51b8ddbc22cf5b10dd76dd9243924aa66ad7db39 GoVersion: go1.16.3 OsArch: linux/amd64 Version: 3.1.2 ```
Package info (e.g. output of rpm -q podman or apt list podman): Running `pacman -Qs podman`: ``` local/podman 3.1.2-1 Tool and library for running OCI-based containers in pods ```
**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)** Yes **Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):** Hetzner Cloud server. SELinux is disabled for now.

comments.body: I am not sure what the issue is here?

```
$ podman run -dt --name "test" \
    --uidmap 0:1:65536 --uidmap 65536:0:1 --uidmap 65537:65537:1 \
    --gidmap 0:1:65536 --gidmap 65536:0:1 --gidmap 65537:65537:1 \
    docker.io/busybox
```

fails with

```
Error: writing file `/proc/3810/gid_map`: Operation not permitted: OCI permission denied
```

with `/etc/subuid`

```
test:100000:65536
test:975:1
```

even though it shouldn't.

I assume there's an error in Podman calculating the `uid_map` from `/etc/subuid` and `--uidmap` with these parameters. Since there are no requirements documented that `/etc/subuid` and `--uidmap` must be sorted, this is a bug.

@PhrozenByte having multiple rows for same user id in `subuid` or `subgid` is correct, you can have one line for one user.
> with `/etc/subuid`
```sh
test:100000:65536
test:975:1
```

Are you trying have three different user id entry in `subuid` or `subgid`? so you are passing multiple params `uidmap` and `gidmap`?

> Are you trying have three different user id entry in `subuid` or `subgid`? so you are passing multiple params `uidmap` and `gidmap`?

Yes and no. As explained, I'm having two entries in `/etc/subuid` and `/etc/subgid`, but three `--uidmap` resp. `--gidmap` command-line options due to the implicit mapping of the executing user. `podman run` fails unless I sort my mappings in `/etc/subuid` and `/etc/subgid`. Since neither `subuid(5)` nor `podman-run(1)` note that `/etc/subuid` and `/etc/subgid` must be sorted, I assume this to be a bug.

you are hitting this issue in the kernel: https://lore.kernel.org/lkml/20210607110804.qgkpxktig2upzfrk@wittgenstein/T/

I've proposed a patch but it wasn't well received.

There is also a fix in Podman upstream:

With a similar setup as yours:

```
$ podman --version
podman version 3.2.0-rc1
$ podman run  --rm --uidmap 0:1:65536 --uidmap 65536:0:1 --uidmap 65537:65537:1 alpine true
Error: writing file `/proc/2618472/gid_map`: Operation not permitted: OCI permission denied

$ bin/podman --version
podman version 3.2.0-dev
$ bin/podman run  --rm --uidmap 0:1:65536 --uidmap 65536:0:1 --uidmap 65537:65537:1 alpine true
```

I am closing the issue since it is fixed upstream, but feel free to comment further
